title: Multiplex Assay of Variant Effect in Yeast
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c9hrl","text":"Where a scalable yeast-based functional assay has been validated, this protocol describes how to perform a high-throughput screen and subsequently generate a variant effect map using TileSeq","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

 Millipore Sigma: CLS431111-16EA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":37,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8i89t","text":"ChargeSwitch Plasmid Yeast Mini Kit (ThermoFisher: CS10203)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpls0","text":"Qubit dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits (Q32850, Q32851)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i4sj","text":"Phusion Hot Start Flex 2X Master Mix (NEB: M0536S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"db9cv","text":"1 kb Plus DNA Ladder (NEB: N3200S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f982r","text":"Agarose (Millipore Sigma: A9539)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7nqkd","text":"NextSeq 500/550 Mid Output Kit v2.5 (300 Cycles; Illumina: 20024905)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u7jc","text":"NextSeq 500/550 High Output Kit v2.5 (300 Cycles; Illumina: 20024908)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Large scale Yeast Transformation Frozen-EZ Yeast Transformation II Kit (T2001, Zymo) Protocol *If your yeast strain is temperature-sensitive, use 25°C for all steps, otherwise use 30°C. Day 1: Inoculate 5 ml of medium with yeast and shake at 200 rpm at 25°C or 30°C overnight. Day 2: Measure the OD 600 of the overnight yeast culture. Dilute yeast cultures to 4 different starting ODs each in 200 ml of medium in 500 ml flasks and shake at 200 rpm at 25°C or 30°C overnight. Example: OD = 0.06, OD = 0.03, OD = 0.015, OD = 0.005 Day 3: Measure the OD 600 of each flask and use the culture closest to OD = 0.8-1.2 for the remaining steps. Split the 200 ml culture into 50 ml falcon tubes and centrifuge at 500 x g for 5 min. Decant supernatant, resuspend and combine the pellets in a total of 15 mL EZ1 solution per transformation Centrifuge at 500 x g for 5 min. Resuspend the pellet in 1.6 ml EZ2 solution (these are now competent yeast cells). Mix cells with DNA and EZ3 solution as follows and vortex gently: Negative (empty vector): 50 μl competent yeast cells + 500 μl EZ3 solution + 500 ng DNA (empty pDEST vector)Library; Each POPcode Region: 1.5 ml competent yeast cells + 15 ml EZ3 solution + 50μg DNA (POPcode pDEST library) Heat shock cells at 30°C for 45 min with shaking/rotating and mix vigorously by hand every 15 min. (Optional Steps 11-16): Incubate yeast transformations on ice for 30 min. Centrifuge at 4000 rpm for 5 min. Recover in 2xYPAD as follows: Controls add 2 ml 2xYPAD; POPCODE region: add 240 ml 2xYPAD Incubate at 25°C or 30°C for 2-3 hours with shaking at 200 rpm. Centrifuge at 4000 rpm for 5 min. Wash cells 3 times (centrifuge at 4000rpm for 5 min after each wash) with autoclaved H 2 O as follows: Control (3X) with 500 μl ddH 2 O; POPCODE region (3X) with 50 ml ddH 2 O Resuspend pellet in autoclaved H 2 O as follows: Control with 50 μl ddH 2 O; POPCODE region with 2.5 ml ddH 2 O Plate culture for efficiency counting as follows, and incubate plates at 25°C or 30°C for 2 days: Control is entire reaction on selective medium; for POPCODE region do 5 μl and 10 μl on selective medium Note: You are expecting at least 500,000 transformants per region For the rest of each POPCODE reaction, measure OD 600 Set up selective liquid media with each entire POPCODE transformation at a starting OD = 0.2 and grow culture in 2L flasks for at least 2 days at 25°C, 30°C or restrictive temperature until the culture reaches saturation. If necessary, split each culture into multiple 2L flasks for better growth and aeration. *This can also be done on by plating OD = 0.2 on media using 500 cm 2 Corning square bioassay dishes Day 5/6: Count efficiency plates for each POPCODE library and calculate the library complexity by taking the average of two dilutions. Non-Selection: If the transformation efficiency was sufficient (≥ 500,000), save 40 OD of each pool for glycerol stocks in duplicate to store at -80°C and 36 OD pellets of each pool for yeast minipreps in duplicate to store at -20°C. Selection: From the selection culture, similarly save 40 OD of each pool for glycerol stocks in duplicate to store at -80°C and 36 OD pellets of each pool for yeast minipreps in duplicate to store at -20°C.
Yeast Plasmid Mini-Prep Protocol Two optional/alternative approaches: Zymoprep and ChargeSwitch

Performed with Zymoprep Yeast Plasmid Miniprep II Kit For each region, aliquot 4 OD of the yeast cells (select and nonselect conditions) into 1.5 ml microfuge tubes Spin down the cells at 600 x g for 2 min. Decant media and resuspend the pellet in 200μL Solution 1-enzyme mixture (containing 15μl of Zymolyase‱) Resuspend the pellet by flicking with finger or mild vortexing. Incubate at 37°C for 60 min Add 200μl Solution 2 to each tube. Mix well by pipetting vigorously. Add 400μl Solution 3 to each tube. Mix well by pipetting vigorously. Centrifuge at maximum speed (13,000 rpm) for 10 min at 4°C. Transfer the supernatant to the Zymo-Spin-I column. Spin the Zymo-Spin I column for 30 seconds at 13,000 rpm Discard the flow-through in the collection tube. Add 550μl of Wash Buffer (ethanol added) onto the column with the collection tube and spin for 1 min at 13,000 rpm. Discard the wash buffer. Place column into a new 1.5 ml microfuge tube Add 100μl PCR grade H 2 O and incubate for 10 min at room temperature Spin down at 13,000 rpm *Yeast plasmid extraction has low efficiency: 1-50 ng/µl purified DNA

Performed with ChargeSwitch Plasmid Yeast Mini Kit from ThermoFisher Transfer 1 ml overnight culture (~2 x 107 cells/ml) to a sterile microcentrifuge tube. Centrifuge at room temperature for 10 seconds at 13,000 rpm. Discard the supernatant. Resuspend the cell pellet in 300 µl Yeast Spheroplast Mix and mix by pipetting. Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes. Add 300 µl Lysis Buffer and invert the capped tube 5 times to mix. Incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes. Add 300 µl chilled Precipitation Buffer and invert the capped tube 5 times or until a white, free-flowing precipitate has formed. Centrifuge at 4°C for 10 minutes at ~12,000 x g. Carefully transfer the supernatant to a sterile labeled tube. Vortex the tube containing the ChargeSwitch Magnetic Beads Add 40 µl ChargeSwitch Magnetic Beads to the lysate Pipette to mix without forming bubbles and incubate at room temperature for 1 minute. Place the sample on the MagnaRack‱ Magnetic Rack for 1 minute or until the beads have formed a tight pellet. Remove and discard the supernatant Remove the tube containing the pelleted magnetic beads from the MagnaRack‱. Add 1 ml Wash Buffer to the tube and mix by pipetting Place the sample on the MagnaRack Magnetic Rack for 1 minute or until the beads have formed a tight pellet. Remove and discard the supernatant Add 1 ml Wash Buffer to the tube and mix by pipetting Place the sample on the MagnaRack Magnetic Rack for 1 minute or until the beads have formed a tight pellet. Remove and discard the supernatant Remove the tubes from the MagnaRack. Add 100 µl Elution Buffer to the tube and mix by pipetting Incubate at room temperature for 1 minute. Place the sample in the MagnaRack for 1 minute or until the beads have formed a tight pellet. Without removing the tube from the MagnaRack, carefully remove the supernatant containing the DNA to a sterile microcentrifuge tube. Nanodrop or HS Qubit to obtain concentration *Yeast plasmid extraction has low efficiency: 1-50 ng/µl purified DNA

Truseq Indexing Procedure *Requires i5 and i7 Truseq oligos, premixed in plates (see attached) %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s Analyze 4μl for each PCR reaction on homemade 2% agarose gel. If all samples are evenly present on gel, pool samples in equal volume to a total volume of 100μl. (eg. For 5 indexed tiles, 20μl each x 5 = 100μl) Run a 4% EX eGel, 5 lanes, 20ul in each lane for each pooled library. Gel purify and minelute to 15μl. Quantify the purified PCR pool via HS Qubit. Dilute the sample to ~5ng/μl. You need a total volume of 30μl here of the diluted samples to have enough for sequencing run. Sequencing done via Illumina runs obtaining 400M (high output kits) or 130M reads (mid output kits)

Pipeline for Functional Score Determination Quantify variant allele frequencies using TileSeq_MutCount: ( https://github.com/RyogaLi/tileseq_mutcount ) Generate scores, and QC analysis, using tileseqMave ( https://github.com/jweile/tileseqMave )

Tiling Procedure: Measuring mutations introduced requires accurate detection of very low allele frequencies, which can be challenged by the base-calling error of Illumina or other NGS platforms. However, base-calling errors can be dramatically reduced by sequencing both strands of short template amplicons (~150bp) that ' tile' the complete coding region, and only calling variants that can be detected on both strands. Forward+Reverse Oligos required for this step – custom primers for each gene for X number of tiles %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s Analyze 4μl for each PCR reaction on homemade 2% agarose gel. Use 1μl (of 1/10 diluted) directly for indexing.
creator: Warren van Loggerenberg
